speaker: ORSINO
text: If music be the food of love, play on.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Give me excess of it, that, surfeiting,

speaker: ORSINO
text: The appetite may sicken and so die.

speaker: ORSINO
text: That strain again! It had a dying fall.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, it came o’er my ear like the sweet sound

speaker: ORSINO
text: That breathes upon a bank of violets,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Stealing and giving odor. Enough; no more.

speaker: ORSINO
text: ’Tis not so sweet now as it was before.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O spirit of love, how quick and fresh art thou,

speaker: ORSINO
text: That, notwithstanding thy capacity

speaker: ORSINO
text: Receiveth as the sea, naught enters there,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Of what validity and pitch soe’er,

speaker: ORSINO
text: But falls into abatement and low price

speaker: ORSINO
text: Even in a minute. So full of shapes is fancy

speaker: ORSINO
text: That it alone is high fantastical.

speaker: CURIO
text: Will you go hunt, my lord?

speaker: ORSINO
text: What, Curio?

speaker: CURIO
text: The hart.

speaker: ORSINO
text: Why, so I do, the noblest that I have.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, when mine eyes did see Olivia first,

speaker: ORSINO
text: Methought she purged the air of pestilence.

speaker: ORSINO
text: That instant was I turned into a hart,

speaker: ORSINO
text: And my desires, like fell and cruel hounds,

speaker: ORSINO
text: E’er since pursue me.

speaker: ORSINO
text: How now, what news from her?

speaker: VALENTINE
text: So please my lord, I might not be admitted,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But from her handmaid do return this answer:

speaker: VALENTINE
text: The element itself, till seven years’ heat,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: Shall not behold her face at ample view,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: But like a cloistress she will veilèd walk,

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And water once a day her chamber round

speaker: VALENTINE
text: With eye-offending brine—all this to season

speaker: VALENTINE
text: A brother’s dead love, which she would keep fresh

speaker: VALENTINE
text: And lasting in her sad remembrance.

speaker: ORSINO
text: O, she that hath a heart of that fine frame

speaker: ORSINO
text: To pay this debt of love but to a brother,

speaker: ORSINO
text: How will she love when the rich golden shaft

speaker: ORSINO
text: Hath killed the flock of all affections else

speaker: ORSINO
text: That live in her; when liver, brain, and heart,

speaker: ORSINO
text: These sovereign thrones, are all supplied, and filled

speaker: ORSINO
text: Her sweet perfections with one self king!

speaker: ORSINO
text: Away before me to sweet beds of flowers!

speaker: ORSINO
text: Love thoughts lie rich when canopied with bowers.
